core_claim: GreenPlasma is a Windows privilege escalation that abuses link following in the Collaborative Translation Framework. The attacker plants an Object Manager symbolic link on a CTF session object and a registry link to redirect a SYSTEM operation. Released by Nightmare Eclipse as their fifth exploit, patched on June 2026 Patch Tuesday.

caption:
Nightmare Eclipse released GreenPlasma (CVE-2026-45586) as their fifth exploit. It is a local privilege escalation that elevates an unprivileged user to SYSTEM by abusing the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework, the input subsystem behind ctfmon.

The class is link following (CWE-59). The attacker places an Object Manager symbolic link on a CTF session object, then abuses a volatile registry link under the CloudFiles BlockedApps key to redirect a privileged write. A forced UAC prompt wakes a Winlogon process running as SYSTEM, and that process follows the planted links instead of the real targets.

No memory corruption. No exploit binary on disk. The released proof of concept is deliberately incomplete and stops short of the final SYSTEM shell. The primitive is confirmed on Windows 11, Server 2022, and Server 2026 before the June 2026 fix.

Defenders miss it because the SYSTEM process is doing exactly what it normally does. The strongest signal is the registry artifact itself: a volatile symbolic link created under HKCU CloudFiles BlockedApps, pointing into the Policies System key.

Deploy the June 2026 Patch Tuesday update. Alert on that registry symlink creation. Watch for Object Manager symlinks placed on Collaborative Translation Framework session objects, and treat an unusual Winlogon desktop switch from an unprivileged process as worth a look.

The patch closes this path. The pattern of redirecting a trusted process through a link it blindly resolves keeps coming back. Hunt the technique, not just the CVE.

A trusted SYSTEM process is only as safe as the names it resolves. A symbolic link the attacker controls turns that trust into a weapon.

Do you alert on registry and Object Manager symlink creation?

Follow for AI security breakdowns without the fake panic.

[GreenPlasma, CVE-2026-45586, Nightmare Eclipse, Windows zero-day, SYSTEM LPE, link following, CTFMON, symbolic link, registry link, defender takeaway]
comment_prompt: Do you alert on registry and Object Manager symlink creation?
alt_text: Title card: GreenPlasma redirects a SYSTEM process with a symbolic link. CVE-2026-45586 is a local privilege escalation that abuses link following in the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework, Nightmare Eclipse exploit five, patched June 2026. A green plasma tendril wraps around a process icon and turns it into a SYSTEM token.

GreenPlasma was the fifth exploit Nightmare Eclipse released. CVE-2026-45586 elevates a local user to SYSTEM by abusing the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework. Patched June 2026 Patch Tuesday, but it was already public before the fix. A glowing process sphere lifts toward a crown of light.

A SYSTEM process follows a symbolic link the attacker planted. A Winlogon process running as SYSTEM resolves a Collaborative Translation Framework object that the local user redirected, with no memory corruption needed, confirmed on Windows 11 and Server 2022 before June 2026. A green tendril threads into a glowing binary orb without breaking its surface.

Two planted links redirect a privileged SYSTEM file operation. The attacker places an Object Manager symbolic link on a Collaborative Translation Framework session object and a volatile registry link under the CloudFiles key, then a UAC prompt wakes the SYSTEM process. A green tendril curves from a bright core toward an outer ring of dim open space.

The SYSTEM process behaves normally while following the links, so endpoint detection misses it. The clearest signal is the registry artifact, a volatile symbolic link created under the CloudFiles BlockedApps key. Two glowing orbs with green verification halos and a thin red thread rising between them.

Apply the patch, then hunt the symbolic link artifacts. Deploy the June 2026 update, alert on a volatile registry link under the CloudFiles BlockedApps key, and watch for Object Manager symlinks on Collaborative Translation Framework session objects. Two process orbs, one with a full shield ring and one with a gap in its ring.

A trusted SYSTEM process is only as safe as the names it resolves. A symbolic link the attacker controls turns that trust into a weapon. A glowing trusted process orb with a green symbolic-link arrow bending its path toward an attacker-controlled target node.
Closing question: Do you alert on registry and Object Manager symlink creation? A single glowing process orb sits on a clean dark end-card with a question-mark shimmer above it.

sources.supports: Official CVE record; CWE-59 improper link resolution before file access (link following) in Windows Collaborative Translation Framework; local elevation of privilege by an authorized attacker; CVSS 7.8 HIGH; MSRC vendor advisory and patch reference; updated 2026-06-10.

sources.source: Blackfort Technology: GreenPlasma — Arbitrary Section Creation on Windows, Analysis and Detection
sources.supports: Mechanism: Object Manager symbolic link placed on CTF.AsmListCache.FMPWinlogon session object plus registry link abuse (volatile REG_LINK under HKCU CloudFiles BlockedApps with SymbolicLinkValue pointing to Policies\System); Winlogon SYSTEM context follows the symlink after a forced UAC desktop switch; PoC published May 12 2026, deliberately incomplete; affected Windows 11, Server 2022, Server 2026; detection via registry-link IOCs.

sources.source: SecurityWeek: Researcher Drops YellowKey, GreenPlasma Windows Zero-Days
sources.supports: Chaotic Eclipse / Nightmare Eclipse released GreenPlasma as a privilege escalation to SYSTEM with the full-shell code stripped out; PoC creates an arbitrary memory section object in a directory writable by SYSTEM; researcher history of dropping unpatched Windows zero-days; Microsoft investigating.

sources.source: ThreatLocker: What YellowKey and GreenPlasma reveal about trusting native Windows security
sources.supports: GreenPlasma (CVE-2026-45586) targets CTFMON, a process running as SYSTEM in every interactive session; fixed on June 2026 Patch Tuesday (June 10 2026) alongside YellowKey; PoC released May 13 2026 before any patch; exploit manipulates registry and object manager permissions to plant a section object and trick CTFMON into interacting with it.

narration.text: GreenPlasma is a Windows privilege escalation that elevates a normal user all the way to SYSTEM.
It was the fifth exploit Nightmare Eclipse dropped, tracked as CVE-2026-45586, and it was already public when Microsoft patched it on June Patch Tuesday.
The bug is link following in the Collaborative Translation Framework, the subsystem behind the ctfmon input process, and the fix class is improper link resolution.

The attacker plants an Object Manager symbolic link on a translation framework session object and a volatile registry link under the CloudFiles key, then forces a prompt that wakes a Winlogon process running as SYSTEM.
That trusted process follows the planted links instead of the real targets, so endpoint tools see nothing unusual while the redirect quietly hands over SYSTEM.
Apply the June 2026 update, and then alert on the strongest tell, a volatile registry symbolic link created under the CloudFiles BlockedApps key.
The lesson is that a trusted SYSTEM process is only as safe as the names it resolves, and a symbolic link the attacker controls turns that trust into a weapon.
Do you alert on registry and Object Manager symlink creation? Drop a yes or no in the comments.

beats.caption: GreenPlasma redirects a SYSTEM process. Symlink.
Patched June. Public before patch. CTF framework.
SYSTEM process follows the planted symbolic link.
Object Manager symlink plus registry link redirect.
Process looks normal. Registry link is the tell.
Patch. Alert on CloudFiles registry symlink creation.
Trust is only as safe as the names it resolves.
Do you alert on symlink creation?
